entry.title: Do you know the different extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Enhancing Key User & Side-by-Side Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview 
This LJ covers two important extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA that are available to customers and partners:

  • Key-User extensibility focuses on adaptations created by key users that have a deep business knowledge. Key-User extensibility comprises adding new fields or custom logic, adapting the standard UIs, creating custom UIs, and creating custom business objects.
  • Side-by-side extensibility on SAP Business Technology Platform allows developers to build their own applications, enhancing and supplementing existing SAP S/4HANA (on premise) apps and SAP S/4HANA Cloud apps.

Learning objectives

  • Get to know about the extensibility concept in SAP S/4HANA
  • Personalize the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Manage the SAP Fiori launchpad content
  • Extend the SAP Fiori launchpad
  • Use variant management
  • Use SAP Fiori runtime authoring
  • Create data source extensions
  • Create custom fields
  • Enable custom database fields for usage in SAP Fiori applications
  • Create custom logic
  • Create custom business objects
  • Create a UI for a custom business object
  • Add custom logic to a custom business object
  • Create custom libraries and methods
  • Create custom code lists
  • Transport extension items
  • Describe essential features of side-by-side extensions
  • Name the most important factors that influence the choice of the extensibility option
  • Develop an extension for SAP S/4HANA with SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Prerequisites

  • Introduction to Application Programming on SAP 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas 3.0 SP19 enhances usability, adds customization options, streamlines workflows
entry.content:
During our monthly SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum meetings, we listen closely to your feedback and insights. As always, customer requests guide our innovations in SAP Screen Personas, ensuring the tool evolves to meet your specific needs. With SP19, we're thrilled to introduce a set of new features designed to further enhance usability, streamline customization, and support efficient workflows.  

Here’s what’s new in SP19!

  • Use multiple table columns to set up conditional formatting.
  • A new option to disable the software keyboard for the relevant input fields.
  • A new option in the Label control and Button controls that allows you to adjust the icon size. (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)
  • You can now introduce toast messages in scripting  (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)

As a reminder, SAP Screen Personas support now runs for two years for each service pack. So, SP19 will remain in support through November 2026. We are supporting SAP Screen Personas functionality in SAP S/4HANA through at least 2040. If you are on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Screen Personas capabilities are embedded as Adapt UI for Classic Apps; nothing to install; it’s ready for use.

Use multiple table columns to set up conditional formatting.

Conditional formatting allows you to highlight a row, based on its contents. We created this feature in an early version of SAP Screen Personas in response to a customer request to highlight line items that contained radioactive components as they were subject to additional regulations. Customers also use this for more mundane uses to flag rows that exceed certain values or contain specific text.

With SP19, we add the ability to compare two columns to set the formatting for a row, based on a condition. For example, if the value in Column1 is “Greater Than” the value in Column2, highlight the row in red.

To include a second column in conditional formatting, you use the same rule creation window. The last column now provides a dropdown menu to select a column for comparison, or you can enter a “Comparison value” as before.

A new option to disable the software keyboard for the relevant input fields.

This feature is designed to enhance user experience in applications that rely on automated input methods, such as scanner devices, barcode readers, and RFID readers. The Advanced Property Editor now includes a new Boolean property, hideSoftwareKeyboard, for GuiTextField and GuiCTextField control types. By setting hideSoftwareKeyboard to TRUE, you can prevent the on-screen keyboard from appearing when these fields are selected. This avoids unnecessary interruptions in workflows where physical or automated data entry is used, eliminating the need for the software keyboard.

This property is especially beneficial in high-efficiency environments such as retail, warehousing, and manufacturing, where input fields are populated through scanning instead of manual typing. By minimizing unnecessary distractions and reducing screen clutter, hideSoftwareKeyboard streamlines operations, resulting in a faster and more seamless data entry process. Additionally, it helps maintain a cleaner interface, allowing operators to focus on tasks without extra steps to dismiss the keyboard, ultimately supporting a more efficient and user-friendly workflow.

A new option in the Label control and Button controls that allows you to adjust the icon size. (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)

A new property called iconFontSize is now available forLabel and Button controls having only icon. This editable property enables you to customize the icon's display size directly within the label component, providing greater control over the icon's visual prominence and alignment. By you can enhance readability, emphasize important elements, or ensure consistency across different screen sizes and resolutions. This update is particularly useful for creating more polished and user-friendly interfaces, allowing for finer adjustments that align with the overall design aesthetic.

 

 

You can now introduce toast messages in scripting (Available only in the Slipstream Engine)

A toast message is a small, non-disruptive popup message that gives the user some information and then disappears automatically after a few seconds. In SP19, a new scripting API command has been introduced for displaying non-interactive popup messages similar to the "messageToast" in SAPUI5. Previously, there was no equivalent API in the Screen Personas script editor for creating toast notifications that don’t require user interaction. With this new command, you can now easily implement message toasts for simple scenarios, allowing for streamlined feedback that aligns with SAP Fiori standards. This addition helps enhance user experience by providing subtle, non-disruptive messages in SAP Screen Personas.

Example:  session.utils.showMessageToast("This is a toast message");

SP19 related notes:

  • SP19 release note contains all the kernel pre-requisites: https://me.sap.com/notes/3525171
  •  SP19 client note:  https://me.sap.com/notes/3525180

Your feedback matters!

These updates with SP19 reflect our commitment to helping you use SAP Screen Personas to simplify classic apps to suit the unique demands of your operations, regardless of industry or role. By adding new functionality, improving customization options, and ensuring stability, SP19 enables you to build a more efficient, user-centric SAP experience. We’re excited to see how these enhancements will support your ongoing success and look forward to continuing to bring your ideas to life.

Thank you for your feedback and participation in our forums your insights are invaluable as we strive to make SAP Screen Personas even more powerful and responsive to your needs.

You already own SAP Screen Personas
We still receive questions about whether there’s an additional license fee for SAP Screen Personas. There’s no extra charge—SAP Screen Personas is already included with your ERP license. Thousands of customers are using SAP Screen Personas to streamline screens in their classic apps on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA, and ECC. Changes made with SAP Screen Personas are upgrade-safe, allowing you to build flavors while keeping a clean core.

Learn more about SAP Screen Personas

  • Online training. The best way to learn the basics about SAP Screen Personas (Adapt UI for classic applications) is to take our free training course on the learning.sap.com platform: https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas
  • Connect with your peers. Every other month, we have a group of customers that gather online to share best practices, demo their flavors, and answer each other’s questions. Experts from the product team also join to explain how to use the latest functionality. If you are interested in joining, you can sign up online to join the SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum. This will add you to the mailing list for the monthly meeting summaries. Please contact “Peter Spielvogel“ or me directly if you want to join the monthly calls.
  • Product documentation. The SAP Screen Personas documentation, recently updated to be even more clear and comprehensive, contains the latest information about the product.

Going forward with SAP Screen Personas

  1. If you are on SP17 or earlier, we recommend you upgrade now, ideally going directly to SP19.
  2. If you are not currently using SAP Screen Personas, why not?
    Worried that we won’t support it into the future? Don’t be.  We are supporting SAP Screen Personas functionality through at least 2040.
    Have other concerns about the roadmap for SAP Screen Personas? See if we answer them in our latest roadmap blog post.the ability to adapt the UI of classic apps is something important to us.
    Have other concerns about the roadmap for SAP Screen Personas? See if we answer them in our latest roadmap blog post.

entry.title: S/4 HANA Cloudification Guide for Developer: Navigating to Released APIs from Classic Objects
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog aims to provide a simple guide for discovering newly released APIs that replace traditional SAP objects, including tables, function modules, class objects, and more.

Whether your organization is implementing Clean Core mindset in S4/HANA On-remise or going through cloud migration(SAP RISE Private Cloud/Public Cloud), one of the first steps towards cloudification is to assess the existing custom solutions are clean core compatible. 

The 3-tier extensibility model is a classification that provides rough idea of how much risk of system upgrade will disrupt your custom solution and how protected and stable it is. In the prerequisite of Tier 1, which by definition has the smallest risk of getting affected by upgrade disruption, states the enforced usage of released APIs. Using released objects is a must and a first step towards clean core way of building custom solutions in your SAP system. 

The challenge with deprecated objects

Before this clean core concept or 3-tier extension model were introduced, the standard way to extend SAP ERP core is using now so-called classic extensibility, which corresponds to the 3rd-tier in the 3tier-extensibility model. Organizations operating with SAP before this new concept are most likely full of classic extensibility solution. If their system is rated with today's clean core standard, the result will probably say it's not cloud ready/compatible. Number of SAP objects used in the classic solution are now labeled as deprecated in the new standard, resulting in renovating all the custom solution that uses the deprecated objects. To adapt to the new standard, it is crucial to map the deprecated objects to the new released APIs to ensure the functionality and the performance of the custom solutions.

(This is, of course, not customer's fault as back then the solutions were probably implemented with the newest technology, insight and standard of that time.)

With this in mind, let's look at what's the easiest way to find released APIs for each classic ABAP objects.

1. Classic objects ➡️ Released objects   

Database Tables

The easiest way is to use Fiori App: View Browser(F2170). Check out ➡️ Finding released CDS views for classic SAP tables  for detailed steps to find released CDS views for classic SAP tables. 

The second easiest way is to use SAP Cloudification Repository, which is also covered in the blog above (however not all objects are listed).

Function Modules / Class

Open the function module in question with ABAP Development Tool(ADT). Open Properties->API State and the successor object is specified.

Example below checks for function module BAPI_PO_CHANGE and class object CL_SD_DOCUMENT_FLOW_RT.

 

 

Alternatively, you can use SAP Cloudification Repository(however not all objects are listed).

 

Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) Definitions 

Open the BAdI Definition in question with ABAP Development Tool(ADT). When opening the object, the enhancement spot that uses the BAdI Definition should open and all the BAdI Definitions will be listed. Right click on the BAdI Definition in question -> API State -> Display Use System-Internally.

Example below checks for BAdI Definition MEOUT_BAPI_CUST.

Release state is shown as well as the successor objects if you click Next.

 

 

 

Function Interfaces, Authorization Object, Data Types..etc.

Successor for rest of objects can be found in ADT same way as function module and class objects.   

 

2. Exploring released APIs

In ABAP Development Tool(ADT)

ADT offers tree views to list all the objects depending on the object release status. You can also customize the tree as you see fit by adding Application Component, Packages and Software Component as an extra layer. It does not only show the released objects but also deprecated objects as well as deprecated objects with successors. 

Check out ➡️ Exploring released APIs in ABAP Development Tool for step-by-step guide. 

 

 

Trees that show Released Objects, Deprecated Objects and Deprecated Objects with Successors

 

In SAP Accelerator Hub

SAP Accelerator Hub is an alternative to find API's for those that don't have developer access and want to explore APIs from business context. It contains not just APIs for tier1/tier2 cloud development, but also out-of-box template for business processes, integration scenarios, events based solutions and everything you need for extension your SAP ERP system. Choose your product(S/4 Public/Private Edition, BTP, etc) and start exploring.

Filter the package and explore released APIs with your desired business area.  

 

Appendix

Released APIs

APIs marked as released are indication that they are technically stable and is the only option available in tier 1 extension to communicate with SAP backend. SAP categorizes the released objects in 5 ways depending on the types and the intension of the extension. In this blog, we focus on the cloudification of SAP objects used for custom development hence will only touch on C1 Contract (Use System-Internally).

  • Extend (C0)
  • Use System-Internally (C1)
  • Use as Remote API (C2)
  • Manage Configuration Content (C3)
  • Use in ABAP-Managed Database Procedures (C4)

C1 Contract (Use System-Internally)

Released in C1 contracts indicate that the object is stable enough to be used in Key-User Extension(Custom fields and Custom Logic apps) and Developer Extensibility(custom solution developed solely by ABAP Development for Cloud language scope). The release state is managed separately for each usage and can be found in ABAP Development Tool.

entry.title: Where can I start learning consumption-based planning in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Using Consumption-Based Planning in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This course provides a comprehensive understanding of consumption-based planning in SAP S/4HANA.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, consultants and business users will be able to:

  • Describe the features and methods of consumption-based planning
  • Explain and control the steps of a planning run specifically for consumption-based planning
  • Perform a planning run and analyze the results
  • Decide on the usage and definition of MRP areas
  • Run a forecast and use the forecast results for planning

Prerequisites
For learners who need to learn the basic procurement business scenarios, please visit the learning journey: 
Exploring the Basics of External Procurement in SAP S/4HANA

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Message no. WR301 "Valuation price of component XXXX in plant YYYY is zero"
entry.content:
During Good Receipt of a prepack article at the Store level leads to an error message 

Valuation price of component XXXX in plant YYYY is zero

Message no. WR301

 

This error message occurs because the component of a prepack at the receiving plant has a Moving Average Price (MAP) of zero. During goods receipt, the system attempts to update the inventory and value of the components. If the MAP is zero, the system triggers this error to prevent incorrect valuations.

Root Cause:

  • When creating or splitting prepack articles, the component's MAP is used in the weighting profile for valuation.
  • A zero MAP leads to an incorrect valuation for the header article.
  • This behavior applies to Display Sets as well and is a standard SAP system process that cannot be modified.

Resolution Steps:

  1. Update Valuation Price:

    • Use transaction MR21 to update the valuation price of the components.
    • Once the MAP has been corrected, the system will allow the goods receipt (GR) to be posted.

  2. Preventative Strategy:

    • Implement a strategy as part of the Master Data setup to ensure components have an MAP during creation.
    • This can be addressed in one of two ways:
      • During Article Master Creation: Ensure MAP values are included when creating articles at the logistics DC or store level.
      • During Listing: Leverage reference handling to ensure MAP values are inherited appropriately during the listing process.

Conclusion:

By ensuring components always have a valid MAP during setup, such errors can be prevented, and smooth processing of goods receipts can be maintained for both prepacks and display sets.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter December 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Activate Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Joule revolutionizes how you interact with your SAP business systems, making every touchpoint count and every task simpler. Would you like to start using on a daily basis? If so, we are piloting a new service to help you with that.
Learn more

Customer Insights Dashboard now Part of SAP for Me

We are excited to announce that the planned transition from the SAP Enterprise Support Reporting Cockpit to the new SAP for Me reporting has been completed and now the new interactive analytical Customer Insights dashboard in SAP for Me is fully replacing the SAP ES reporting cockpit.
All about the move

In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

We’re happy to announce that the product deep-dive enablement series is ready to roll out again. The series aims to provide SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition product deep-dive knowledge, independently from the release update.
Mark your calendar for December 9 to get the latest update.
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Series

E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud BI Story Builder

Check out this new E-Learning focused purely on the basics of developing an efficient and effective BI Story in SAP Analytics Cloud. Included in the Reference Materials section of the E-Learning you can find hands-on materials such as sample data file, sample scripts, tutorial exercises and more.
Details in this blog

New! SAP Design to Operate: Strategy of Each Pillar E-learning

SAP offers solutions and strategy to digitalize and enhance the efficiency of supply chain operations. For insights, we‘re pleased to share SAP Design to Operate: Strategy of Each Pillar e-learning. This E-learning provides an overview of the SAP Design to Operate lines of business, covering Design, Plan, Manufacture, Deliver, and Operate, and offers a comprehensive understanding of end-to-end supply chain operations.
Access the elearning (value maps access required)

SAP S/4HANA Finance Fundamentals – Deep Dive

Join us in a multiday training session to discover everything you need to know about delta capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Finance. We offer two sessions in December, on Dec 2 and 9.
More in this blog post

How to Get Started With SAP S/4HANA Finance

Are you about to start with SAP S/4HANA Finance? Then don’t miss our interactive virtual Live Must-Know session that lasts less than 2 hours on December 9 that helps you get on board.
Register through this blog post: Get started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

SAP’s Built-In Support: When Real-Time Support Meets Compliance for Life Sciences Supply Chain

Built-In Support is changing the way assistance is provided for users in the life sciences supply chain. Want to know how? Don’t miss this interview featuring Tarun Luthra and Andreas Krummlauf about traceability of life-saving medicines and how streamlined tracking processes enhance user experience and support efficiency.
sap.to/61108SzXf4

The Evolution of Customer Support in Software: Harnessing AI and LLMs to Drive Excellence

Explore the future of customer support! Learn how AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) are transforming traditional support, driving proactive solutions, and personalizing experiences. Companies like SAP are leading the way to predict issues, boost response times, and create stronger customer relationships. Check it out to see how AI-powered insights are reshaping Customer Support.
Read the full article in LinkedIn

Introducing Business Impact-Driven Case Prioritization

We wanted to let you know about a recent change we've made in the ‘Get Support’ application from SAP for Me. The key change is the shift from prioritizing issues based on levels to describing the business impact of the issue. This will directly determine the priority of your case, leading to more accurate and responsive support.
sap.to/61100SmsFY

Companies That Get Customer Orientation Right

Customer orientation is vital for retention, especially when things don’t go as planned. In his latest article, Stefan Steinle, Executive Vice President, Head of Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management at SAP, discusses how brands like Singapore Airlines, Amazon, and Swiggy deliver customer-centricity at every step. Their thoughtful service, even during challenges, helps turn customers into advocates. At SAP, a similar approach is taken, where exceptional support helps clients like Audi set new standards for excellence.
Click here for the article

Replay the virtual SAP Enterprise Support Day

Couldn’t make it out to the event? Experience it again! Explore SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024 sessions, presentations and FAQs in a variety of different languages, anytime.
Check out the event recap

  • Guidance on signing up
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document
  • FAQ

  • Basics: Sign up | Time zone setup |
  • Formats: EGI | MTE | Program
  • Search: Language | Keyword | Filter | Calendar
  • Upcoming live sessions in your region: APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America

entry.title: Customize with confidence on the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In my conversations with customers, I keep hearing that extensibility is extremely important to them. It allows their systems to adapt and grow with their businesses and enables customization to fit specific needs and easy integration with other tools, enhancing overall efficiency and user experience. With SAP BTP, we are providing a stable and reliable way to keep innovating and expanding your SAP solution beyond its standard capabilities. This year at SAP TechEd, we announced the general availability of the new SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
 
The Extensibility Wizard is a tool that helps you quickly and easily add new features or customize the standard capabilities of your SAP software to better fit your business needs, without requiring extensive technical expertise.

In this blog, I’ll recap what we shared at SAP TechEd 2024 around the Extensibility Wizard, its main business value for customers, and provide some key resources to help you get started. I’ll also share my thoughts about how this release fits into SAP’s vision for extensibility and reveal a sneak peek at what lies ahead.

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard at TechEd 2024

 

 

 

 

 

The Extensibility Wizard was among this year’s key announcements at SAP TechEd. During the keynote, we also shared the news that ABAP environments are now part of SAP Build, and that customers can develop ERP extensions within a single suite of AI-driven tools. The Extensibility Wizard takes this a step further by bringing the ABAP and non-ABAP worlds together directly within the ERP, making SAP Build the default solution to modernize your core system. See all of this year’s main announcements in the TechEd keynote replay: SAP TechEd 2024 Keynote - Where ideas get real.

In case you missed it, I highly recommend checking out these on-demand sessions featuring the Extensibility Wizard, including strategy, common scenarios, demos and more:

  • AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build
  • AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions

What’s the main business value of the Extensibility Wizard?

From SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 onwards, the wizard is now available in every business application that is enabled for key user adaptation. Both key users and developers can launch the wizard directly from their SAP S/4HANA system to start creating on-stack extensions, as well as side-by-side extensions with BTP and SAP Build. In the future, we’ll plan to enable it in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and on-prem.

See these blogs to catch up on what’s available and how to get started:

  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
  • The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?

 

 

 

So with the Extensibility Wizard readily available, what’s the business value customers can now achieve? Here are the main benefits to know:

  • All extensibility options brought together in one place: The Extensibility Wizard consolidates on-stack extensions that are already possible like custom field and logic, together with side-by-side – bringing BTP and S/4HANA Cloud together in a single extensibility space.
  • Contextualized experience for extension development: One of the wizard’s main capabilities is that it automatically understands the business context of the application you are using and filters the corresponding extension points, such as APIs and events.  For on-stack extensions, it navigates you to the respective business application or key user extensibility apps.
  • Leverages generative AI and other BTP innovations: By providing seamless navigation into SAP Build, now customers and partners can take advantage of the latest BTP capabilities, such as AI agents and prebuilt content, to truly accelerate their ERP modernization.

SAP Build as part of SAP’s overall vision for extensibility

With the release of Extensibility Wizard and the addition of ABAP environments to SAP Build, we are realizing our vision for more accessible extensibility and clean core development.

As the graphic below illustrates, SAP Build provides an entire suite of extensibility options to meet every type of developer persona or business need. From low- to pro-code, backend to frontend development – all of them are under the umbrella of SAP Build.

Just as SAP Build has made app development accessible to citizen developers, the Extensibility Wizard now makes extension creation a smoother and more streamlined experience that is clean core by default.

It guides key users through the first steps, making it easier to start and know which tools to use. Professional developers no longer need to start from scratch, as they can leverage features like gen AI and maintained, prebuilt content. SAP Build continues to enable fusion development from multiple angles, making it a truly holistic portfolio for extensibility.

Want to learn more about our vision for extensibility?

  • See five use cases of SAP Build extending SAP S/4HANA for finance, supply chain and other lines of business in the eBook.
  • Explore our overall strategy for clean core and extensibility model in this whitepaper.

Looking ahead to a new Extensibility era

With the Extensibility Wizard and our other TechEd milestones, we’re thrilled to take extensibility into a new era, where the worlds of BTP, S/4HANA, and ABAP come closer together! Developers and business users alike will be able to extend and innovate their core systems based on their specific skill sets and custom needs. And it’s only the beginning.

This visual illustrates how SAP Build encompasses the development of enterprise-ready extensions. You’ll note that generative AI is a centerpiece of the portfolio, with many new capabilities announced at TechEd to further accelerate the development process. AI is already changing how we interact with technology and develop software, and it’s a trend that will continue to shape the entire industry.

So, what’s next for the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard?

Currently, it’s possible to create custom fields, logic, and process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The option to create custom apps with SAP Build Code is on the roadmap.

We’re also pleased to announce that the Extensibility Wizard is targeted for SAP SuccessFactors in the future, offering a more robust and standardized way to enhance core HR systems. The Extensibility Wizard will operate with the same behavior and framework as it does in SAP S/4HANA, integrating SAP Build within Employee Center with numerous event hooks available.

For a sneak peek at how this will look, check out the announcement from this year’s SAP SuccessConnect event in Lisbon by Siva Sundaresan, SVP of Applications Engineering at SAP SuccessFactors. He also shares a demo of the Extensibility Wizard in the Onboarding application: Road Map and Vision Session (starts around 15:45).

***

Hopefully this got you inspired and ready to kick off your transformation journey with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard. There are plenty more capabilities to come, so stay tuned!

For additional learning, see these Devtoberfest sessions and demos:

  • S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard
  • What software developers want to know about Key User Extensibility
  • Demo - Easily extend SAP S/4HANA to create clean, core compliant extensions using the extensibility wizard
  • Demo - GenAI and SAP Build: Accelerate Development Projects

entry.title: Where can I learn the basic configuration settings for delivery documents in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Delivery Processing in SAP S/4HANA Sales learning journey.

Overview
In this learning journey, you can learn the basic configuration settings for delivery documents in SAP S/4HANA. Both inbound and outbound processes are discussed, as well as the integration with SAP EWM.

Learning objectives
This learning journey will prepare you to:

  • Work with delivery documents
  • Maintain organizational units for delivery processing
  • Control deliveries (such as the delivery type and delivery item category determination)
  • Post a goods issue for a delivery
  • Integrate delivery processing with EWM

 

Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Sales 
    or 
  • Applying Supply Chain Execution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Basic Overview on SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Private Edition on Azure
entry.content:
Hi 

Nowadays SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Private Edition is one the deployment option. It is being most demanding. Here I am going to share an Overview on SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Private Edition on Azure. It will be help to keep conceptual knowledge for SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Private Edition on Azure.

Introduction to SAP on Azure

SAP applications form the basis of many enterprise operations, including those of multinational companies. The enterprise resource planning (ERP) applications that SAP provides are highly complex and capable of providing the scalability and analytics that are necessary for many organizations. As organizations move to the cloud it is key that they can take these applications with them which is why Microsoft has partnered with SAP on Azure.

Usage of SAP on Azure

Combining your SAP and Azure operations can provide several benefits that are difficult to obtain with SAP or Azure alone. These include greater flexibility of operations, scalability, and integration of solutions.

Azure Is Hybrid- Azure offers support for hybrid operations beyond connectivity and infrastructure. It enables you to consistently perform operations regardless of platform, including identity management, application development, and security. Native and third-party integrations support these capabilities.

Azure Can Use Bare Metal for HANA-You can deploy HANA to VMs or bare metal servers in Azure. This enables you to ensure that resources are maintained in isolation with dedicated hardware. These servers come built-in with infrastructure for storage, compute, and networking. Additionally, bare metal servers are certified for HANA Tailored Data Center Integration.

Easy Integration of SAP and Microsoft Products-A long term partnership between Microsoft and SAP provides significant support for integrated solutions. For example, both Power BI and Office 365 support the use of SAP HANA. Additionally, both vendors use open standards and interfaces for data exchange, including OData. This enables you to integrate products smoothly with no need for data transformation.

Almost 100% Availability-Azure enables you to spread operations and data redundancies across AZs. Data is automatically replicated for durability and availability. You can also create additional backups or select geo-redundant storage for disaster recovery and failover protection.

Introduction to SAP S/4 Han a Cloud Private Edition and its features

SAP S/4 HANA  Cloud Private Edition is a tailored-to-fit cloud ERP that adapts to your organization's unique transformation. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition safeguards your existing investment in SAP ERP while benefiting from the latest capabilities and greater flexibility.

Running on your standard

  • Maximize your differentiation by tailoring business processes to your unique requirements​​
  • Protect your investment and move to the cloud at your pace

Modernizing your business

  • Achieve bottom-line growth with proven business processes for your industry
  • Deliver green-line growth by building sustainability directly into your business

Keeping control

  • Manage the speed of change by rolling out innovations on your timeline
  • Choose the cloud infrastructure based on your landscape and preferences

SAP S/4 Hana Cloud Private Edition on Azure

The Microsoft Cloud helps you unleash the power of your SAP applications and data. Whether you migrate to SAP S/4HANA on Azure, leverage RISE with SAP for a fully managed service, or add intelligence with Azure data and AI services, Microsoft provides a reliable, secure and comprehensive cloud platform to transform your SAP estate.

SAP solutions on Azure

Run SAP solutions across development, testing, and production scenarios in Azure. SAP on Azure solutions help you optimize your enterprise resource planning (ERP) in the cloud using the security features, reliability, and scalable SAP-certified infrastructure of Azure. This intelligent ERP suite is designed specifically for in-memory computing. Whether you want to run SAP S/4HANA in a new implementation or migrate from SAP Business Suite to SAP S/4HANA, Azure has you covered.

SAP S/4HANA on Azure benefits:

  • Seamless connectivity for end users who need to access SAP Fiori-based applications when using Azure ExpressRoute.
  • A 99.99 percent SLA for your critical ERP instances by running all product instances of SAP S/4HANA in a high-availability pair by running in two Azure Availability Zones.
  • Backup for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BW/4 HANA in seconds, with a low recovery-time objective—even for multi-terabyte databases.

Certification that SAP S/4HANA runs on SUSE Linux Enterprise and Red Hat Enterprise Linux servers. Plus, SAP HANA on Azure Virtual Machines  and SAP HANA on Azure large-instance machines are certified to run SAP S/4HANA. Microsoft Azure is one of the cloud infrastructure options for the RISE with SAP offering. On Azure, RISE with SAP leverages Microsoft’s global network infrastructure to provide connectivity to SAP applications in a secure and reliable way.

SAP S/4HANA on Azure offers:

• SAP on Azure VM workloads
• Azure Center for SAP solutions
• SAP on Azure deployment automation framework
• Azure Monitor for SAP solutions

There are multiple Microsoft Azure offerings for running and managing your SAP systems. These offerings range from traditional Azure virtual machine (VM) offerings to top-level Azure services, to tools that integrate with other Azure services or external products.

SAP on Azure VM workloads

You can run SAP workloads on the Azure platform using different Azure Virtual Machines (Azure VMs) offerings. Azure is certified for multiple SAP products, including SAP HANA and SAP NetWeaver products.

SAP Integration with Microsoft Services-In addition to the capabilities to run SAP IaaS and SaaS workloads on Azure, Microsoft offers various capabilities, scenarios, best-practice guides, and tutorials to integrate SAP workloads running anywhere with other Microsoft products and services. Among them are popular services such as Microsoft Entra ID, Exchange Online, Power Platform and Power BI, Azure Integration Services, Excel, SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, and SAP Success Factors to name a few.

SAP HANA on Azure (Large Instances)-SAP HANA on Azure (Large Instances) is a solution that provides VMs for deploying and running SAP HANA.

Azure Center for SAP solutions

Azure Center for SAP solutions is a service that makes SAP a top-level workload in Azure. This end-to-end solution allows you to create and run SAP systems as a unified workload on Azure. You can use this service through the Azure portal, a REST API, and the Azure CLI.

SAP on Azure deployment automation framework

The SAP on Azure deployment automation framework is an open-source orchestration tool for deploying, installing, and maintaining SAP environments.

Azure Monitor for SAP solutions

Azure Monitor for SAP solutions is an Azure-native monitoring product for SAP landscapes that run on Azure, which uses specific parts of the Azure Monitor infrastructure.

Conclusion - It is secured and SAP Private Link serves as a great 'Private' connectivity option for certain use-cases enabling access to private service endpoints and avoids public endpoints, thereby resulting in transfer of data over private networks.

Reference-  www.SAP.com,

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-in/azure/sap/workloads/hana-vm-operations

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-sap-private-link-and-azure-private-link-quot-in-the-context-of-sap/ba-p/13719685

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/rise-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-secure-cloud-connectivity/ba-p/13558064

entry.title: One Tool to Track Them All
entry.content:
We launched our process-tracking tool, Process Observer, over a decade ago. Since then, a lot has changed. Now, our goal is Cloud First, and we want our customers to RISE to the cloud and GROW in the cloud.

SAP acquired and integrated Signavio a while ago and our biggest question was - what is the future of Process Observer in the cloud and how does it play together with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence? The answer — A new kid on the block, "BUSINESS EVENT LOGGING (BEL)."

BEL uses a much leaner and more performant approach compared to Process Observer. It also requires less configuration on your part while still giving you the freedom to enhance and integrate your processes. At the same time, it allows you to easily integrate with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

 

 Fig. 1: Event Log Viewer App in Business Event Logging

 

Overview and Benefits

So, what is Business Event Logging?

  •  It collects the event data locally and you can use integration to extract the data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.
  • It is based on Business Events, but it is also planned to support other sources like Change Documents.
  • It provides field change information.
  • It is available now in SAP S/4HANA CE, SAP S/4HANA OP/PC and SAP ABAP Platform Cloud.

How can BEL be useful for you?

  • You can activate logging of events just by selecting the relevant Object Types. You can create process views (“cases”) in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and analyze your processes there.
  • You can use the data viewer apps to review logged data in multiple aspects.
  • You can manage the data retention in the business event log with SAP ILM.
  • You can extend processes or log your own processes by creating your own Business Events.

 

The Foundation

Business events are object-centric events created in the RAP framework. They are assigned with an event binding and are C2-released by SAP. You can find these events documented in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, Follow the path: Explore / <your product> / Events.  

 

Fig. 2: Business Events in the BEL Accelerator Hub

The RAP framework is an extensible framework: you can create your own objects, create events with binding, extend SAP delivered objects with your events, or just extend an SAP-delivered event.

Business events can also contain change information. For more information, you can explore event definitions in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This change information is now visible in the BEL log.

If you require additional standard events delivered by SAP, or change information, you can raise a request via the Customer Influence Portal. .

Beyond these Business Events, there are internal events such as RAP events, SAP-delivered C1- events, and Change Documents for other BEL data sources. We plan to support them in the future and will keep you updated.

 

Release support

You might be wondering when you could start using this. The great news is that if you’re in the cloud you can start right away!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition & SAP BTP, ABAP Environment:

For the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 or later and for SAP BTP, ABAP Environment 2411 or later, we support:

  • Logging of all released business events for all object types as listed in the SAP Accelerator Hub for the product.
  • Logging of customer events. For more information, see the instructions in the product documentation.

You can find the detailed documentation for Business Event Logging for these products here:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (latest): User documentation  

SAP BTP, ABAP Environment (latest): User documentation  

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition & On-Premise Edition:

The support in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise Edition depends on the release.

Functionalities provided with PC/OP 2021 FPS2: 

  • Only logging of Order-to-Cash related events provided by SAP (based on Business Event Handling). 
  • Customer extensibility is not supported by Business Event Handling.

Functionalities provided with PC/OP 2022 FPS1: 

  • Only logging of Order-to-Cash related events provided by SAP (based on RAP)  
  • Customer events are supported with FPS 2 of OP 2022

Functionalities provided with PC/OP 2023 FPS2: 

  • Logging of all Business Events provided by SAP is supported (as documented in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub).

Functionalities provided with S/4HANA Private Cloud / On Premise 2023 (latest):

  • You can view the list of functionalities in the User documentation.

We will cover the integration of the component and the customer extensibility in separate articles.

entry.title: Selling Energy Using the Sales Order Integration into Utilities Sales Contracts
entry.content:
Utilities Retailers moving to SAP S/4HANA Utilities can benefit from a new concept by using the SAP S/4HANA sales orders for selling commodity and non-commodity products. This brings many benefits for the integration of different sales channels. Products, prices and sales processes for commodity and non-commodity products can be better managed and out-of-the-box integration is offered to several SAP solutions.

The target of this blog is to describe this new concept, its benefits, how product definition looks like, and which customizing is required.

Basic concept:

Product Definition:

  • Product Managers can easily create new utilities products by copying template reference products and adding customer-related product information (like contract duration, withdrawal periods, renewable energy, target groups, eligibility).
  • Technically, the utilities products are defined as configurable materials and matched to S/4H Utilities Billing Master Data (rate categories, prices, discounts etc.) via the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL). Information required in Utilities like the contract start date, Meter ID or a termination date are added as characteristics via classes to the product using advanced variant configuration.

Order Management:

  • With the sales integration interface, S/4HANA sales order (formerly known as “SD Order”) are used to capture commodity orders for energy, water, gas, or district heating products. Utilities products can be sold together with physical products like wall boxes, heat pumps or smart home devices. Easy product bundling is also supported. Additional contract lifecycle events, such as product change, or contract termination result in creation of a sales order with the desired product(s) as items. The product configurable attributes, such as meter id or point of delivery can be maintained during order creation.
  • Once the SD order is created and saved, further downstream processes are triggered via the Customer Engagement Process Framework. This process framework orchestrates downstream processing, such as initiating the start of supply process via Market Communication. Market Communication process is responsible for communicating to other market participants and will determine whether the contract creation request/date is valid for the current point of delivery.  Upon successful reply from market communication process, utilities technical master data is created, such as premise, installation, which in turn enables the creation of a Utilities sales contract and Utilities contract (IS-U contract).

Diagram: Order-to-cash process

  Screenshot: Sales order creation for a new contract: overview screen

 Screenshot: Sales order creation for a new contract: configuration of an energy item

Benefits:

  1. Streamline commodity order integration: Use the widely used industry-agnostic SAP S/4HANA sales orders as a unified order integration interface for both commodity and non-commodity products, leveraging the vastly available SD knowledge and talent pool to achieve your integration goals.
  2. Many SAP solutions offer standard out-of-the-box integrations into SAP S/4HANA sales orders which are now relevant for Utilities as well. This reduces the implementation effort and offers new customer facing solutions. Examples are:
    • SAP CPQ to apply pricing and generate quotes (SAP BTP based).
    • SAP Advanced Solution Bundling for easy definition of sales bundles (SAP BTP based).
    • SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing to configure and price your products accurately (SAP BTP based). This cloud service can be integrated and embedded into customer facing solutions reducing integration costs with S/4HANA Utilities significantly.
    • SAP S/4HANA Solution Quotation Management for combinations of different types of products (such as commodity products, tangible products, services, and service contract items). These combinations of products can also be modelled as product bundles in your master data. 
  3. Combine in one order commodity products of different divisions like energy, gas and water. 
  4. Enter the market of solution business and sell commodity products in combination with non-commodity products:
    • for physical products via the SAP S/4HANA sales order,
    • for using full functionality of SAP S/4HANA service contracts and orders or subscriptions the S/4HANA solution quotation can be used to capture and manage mixed orders and create in a second step automatically the sales order, service contracts etc.
  5. Avoid creation of unnecessary master data before deregulated start of supply process is completed successfully.
    • In deregulated markets, the SAP S/4HANA sales order can capture the order and the Customer Engagement Process Framework triggers the start of supply process in SAP Application Process Engine (APE) without creating any technical or commercial master data (beyond the business partner) first.
    • These data are only created after successful completion of the market communication process, thus massively reducing the risk of prematurely creating erroneous or unwanted master data.
  6. Product Managers can easily create new products by copying template reference products and adding additional customer related product information.
    • The reference product (also known as UPIL product) ensures via the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL) the integration to the IS-U billing. The reference product will be set up as a template for a certain product group in advance by a billing master data and UPIL specialist.
  7. Via the creation of different Sales Orders contract lifecycle events and processes for the Utilities sales contract can be triggered.
    • Technically, the Sales Order Item Category indicates which event will be raised. The mapping is defined in customizing. Therefore, it is possible to influence existing contracts upon creation of a sales order. This means the sales order will not be changed, but new sales orders will be created to trigger new lifecycle events.
    • Example: For triggering the termination of a Utilities sales contract, a sales order is created (e.g. via API). The sales order has one item with a product and the item category for “terminate contract". Dedicated characteristics to the "product change" event will be entered as characteristics to the product, e.g. the termination / move out date and the reference ID to the Utilities contract.
    • The following lifecycle events are supported:
      • creation of new Utilities sales contracts,
      • change product from product A to product B,
      • change Utilities sales contract product configuration and prices of the given product,
      • change start date,
      • change end date, 
      • withdraw a Utilities sales contract,
      • terminate a Utilities sales contract, e.g. related to a move-out or customer churn.
  • Screenshot: Sales order item with information on Utilities sales contract termination

8. It is possible to define SAP S/4HANA as the leading system for pricing using the pricing engine and its condition records. Prices can be maintained centrally there and can be replicated to SAP and non-SAP systems.

  • SAP S/4HANA sales has a very powerful and flexible pricing that allows to maintain or upload prices. Prices can be maintained e.g. for
    • usage based working prices (per kWh),
    • yearly recuring base prices,
    • percentage based or fixed amount discounts on the working price or recuring price,
    • scale prices that depend e.g. on the consumed kWh.
  • Sales prices are transferred from SAP S/4HANA pricing engine to utilities sales contracts, and billing for SAP for Utilities (IS-U). The billing integration to the utilities contract is performed by the process framework using the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL).
  • Remark: prices representing cost s.a. grid usage or taxes and levies need not necessarily be modelled as S/4HANA condition records but rather in the classic SAP S/4HANA Utilities billing engine. Processes for changing sales and prices and such underlying price components are likely to differ.
  • The integration to the BTP-based SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing service is a major benefit.
  • Remote management of prices and conditions is supported by a rich set of APIs.
  • The handling of regional prices and price zones as required in the German market can be supported e.g. based on the postal code of the supply to addressee. Prices can then be defined for each price zone that combines several postal codes.

Screenshot: Customizing of SAP Utilities Contract Billing -> Product Integration: Mapping product characteristics to operands and condition types

Availability of features:

The first features of the sales integration interface have been delivered with SAP S/4HANA 2023:

  • The objective is to seamlessly integrate sales orders with customer engagement sales contracts, primarily for new contracts. This integration enables configuration and pricing functionalities within sales orders, which are transferred to utilities sales contracts, utilities business add-ins, and billing for SAP for Utilities (IS-U).

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 in February 2024 

  • Integrate the sales orders in SAP S/4HANA with Utilities sales contract management in Customer Engagement to manage the contract lifecycle of the Utilities sales contract.
    • Change product for utilities sales contract via integrated sales orders
    • End utilities sales contract via integrated sales order
  • Automatically determine or create Contract Account
    • With this feature an example BAdI is provided to determine a contract account in the Process Framework of Customer Engagement from bill to party address and payment method and the creation of new contract account if needed (via template).
  • Handover of sale prices to Utilities prices
    • With this feature sale prices are handed over to Utilities prices to achieve pricing consistency between sales order prices and Utilities prices. Automatic adoption of all price changes to Utilities billing is now possible upon creation of a new order.
    • Prices and price changes and discounts on usage based prices or recurring base prices are maintained as condition records in SAP S/4HANA Sales. 
    • CE-Process Framework takes care about the creation of Utilities price keys based on the product information from UPIL.​
    • A selling system shall integrate with SAP S/4HANA to receive the conditions​.
    • Restriction: no amount simulation for time of use or dynamic pricing. 

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 in October 2024

  • Promotion of the following three additional lifecycle events to influence existing contracts upon creation of a sales order: change start date, change end date, and contract withdrawal.
  • Provisioning of rejection reasons in the sales order for error handling

For future features please see SAP Roadmap Explorer: link 1 and link 2

Customizing:

As part of the “SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition” the customizing of the "SAP S/4HANA sales order management and processing" related settings is delivered as standard content: “Sales Integration for SAP S/4HANA Utilities (7JO)”,

  • Process Navigator - SAP for Me

A cookbook on how to customize the sales order and the Sales Integration Interface is provided:

  • Complete List of Cookbooks and Guidelines.pdf
  • see under: SAP S/4HANA Utilities for Customer Engagement -> Sales Integration Interface

entry.title: Which S/4HANA extensibility option is the best for thriving as an SAP partner in the cloud space?
entry.content:
Get more value from S/4HANA with SAP Extensibility options, part 3

In my first article   of this series, I explained why extensibility options are crucial for S/4HANA customers. In the second article , I explained which option I should choose for the different use cases from a customer's point of view. In this last article, I want to focus on existing and new SAP partners who wish to connect their cloud offerings to S/4HANA customers or migrate their existing SAP ECC add-on to ABAP Cloud.

I have worked for almost 30 years in the SAP ERP space and have seen many innovations in SAP software. I started in 1996 when SAP introduced the client-server ERP and RFC libraries. During the turn of the century, SAP adopted the Internet and launched its new dimension portfolio, and third-party integration products were introduced into the market. SAP has stepped into the cloud in the last decade and fully adopted artificial intelligence.

There are a lot of changes at the core of ERP, but the challenges in my job are almost the same. As a developer, enterprise architect, SAP mentor, speaker, teacher, and advisor, I ask myself, ‘How can I add additional value for my customers out of an ERP system?’ And the answer is always the same. Focus on the missing functionality. SAP always delivers best-practice solutions that never fulfill all customers' requirements. This is still an ample opportunity for me, consultancy companies, SAP add-on solution providers, and all other software vendors.

For the last eight years, I have mostly worked for big cloud companies like Blackline, Booking, and Zuora to advise them to connect their cloud portfolio and systems to SAP S/4HANA. The cloud companies' customers are also big SAP customers. In this article, I will share my experience and tell you which S/4HANA extensibility options fit the best cloud vendors that want to thrive as SAP partners.

Challenges for SAP Solution Partners

The question is easy, but the answer is more complex. As I wrote in my previous article, SAP supports several S/4HANA extensibility options, which depend on the S/4HANA releases and versions. So you need to answer the question: Do you want to support the S/4HANA Cloud private version, the S/4HANA Cloud public version, or even support ‘classic’ SAP ECC?  But also, do you want to build a standalone add-on that runs on Business Technology Platform Software as a Service (BTP SAAS) or is embedded on S/4HANA? Or do you want to connect your partner cloud solution to S/4HANA?

But there are more answers needed. The data and events must be publicly available in the S/4HANA system. How will we handle the data exchange's latency, performance, size, and security? Who will be responsible when something goes wrong, technical, infra, and business-wise? Will the current SAP license be enough, or will there be additional operational and license costs for my customers? How will user management, authentication, authorization, and security be handled? How will the execution be monitored, and how will alerting and mapping be arranged? How should sensitive data be managed, and what audit trails and data should be archived? How will networking, security, risk, and complaint departments react to the solution? Will the used platform and tooling be future-proof? And so we can ask a lot more questions.

Ultimately, all these questions should be answered to develop the best solution. However, in my experience, not all SAP partners consider these questions.

SAP positions the S/4HANA Cloud public edition as the preferred version to GROW with SAP in the long term. However, SAP also provides the S/4HANA Cloud private edition for existing customers to RISE, with SAP as the golden path to move to the S/4HANA Cloud public edition in the future.

We will first focus on the S/4HANA Cloud public edition because this release is SAP’s long-term solution. Next, we can consider other SAP ERP systems, such as the S/4HANA Cloud private edition, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP ECC.

S/4HANA extensibility options

In my second article, I described the eight extensibility options for S/4HANA. However, if we look at partner solutions, we can skip the classic extensibilities because they are not supported in the public edition and are obsolete from a certification point of view. Also, the low-code extensibilities options S/4HANA Key User Extensibility, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Build Apps could be more suitable because they mainly focus on custom developments for the citizen developer.

Figure 1 – Possible Partner Integration Options

 

This limited the possible extensibility options, and because we can also access the S/4HANA APIs and events directly, we will have five possibilities (See Figure 1) to deliver a partner solution:

  • An add-on application on the S/4HANA embedded ABAP environment
  • A partner application as a SAAS application running on SAP BTP ABAP environment
  • A partner application as a SAAS application developed with SAP Build Code on SAP BTP
  • An integration package on SAP Cloud Integration
  • Running your application on a non-SAP solution and directly accessing S/4HANA events and APIs.

Using APIs and events

Software vendors that run their solutions on their cloud will primarily consider the last option and try to connect their cloud solution directly to S/4HANA events and APIs. But from my experience, this isn’t the right approach. The S/4HANA Cloud public edition strongly depends on business events, and most cloud vendor solutions aren’t prepared for this. Due to the configuration possibilities in the S/4HANA Cloud and the use of APIs, the responsibility for root cause analysis shifts from the customer to the cloud vendor, which needs to build a big SAP knowledge support center. Also, the cloud vendor should handle all security, risk, and compliance issues, which are initially the customer's responsibility.

Another issue with this approach is that you, as a partner, cannot certify the solution with SAP. SAP is not responsible for the solution's availability and performance, code changes, or deprecation of APIs and events. This approach carries a high risk for customers and the cloud vendor.

SAP Cloud Integration

A better approach is an integration package that can be installed on the customer's SAP Cloud Integration system. In this case, SAP will ensure smooth integration between S/4HANA and SAP Cloud Integration. The solution can be certified, and as a cloud vendor, you can mainly focus on integrating your cloud partner solution and SAP Cloud integration. The downside of this approach is that SAP Cloud Integration is separate from any S/4HANA Cloud edition license and should be brought by the customer separately. Only some customers are willing to do so. Most customers already have an existing integration product for more than just their SAP landscape. The need for separate SAP Cloud Integration licenses and setting up a support and knowledge center to maintain SAP Cloud Integration will be a blocker. Providing an integration package as a partner solution will limit the number of SAP customers you can connect to your cloud solution in the market.

ABAP Cloud environments

The best approach for cloud vendors is to build the connectivity as an add-on product on S/4HANA, a SAAS solution on BTP, or a combination of these options. Building your partner integration solution on an ABAP Cloud environment is the best extensibility option for applications strongly dependent on S/4HANA. Based on my investigation, cloud vendors will profit the most from this option. They can shift the responsibility of the availability and maintenance of the landscape to SAP; they are highly flexible in a robust and stable environment following SAP’s future path and can directly profit from the innovations SAP is launching. The barriers to developing these extensions are higher, but the barriers for customers will be lower. There will be no additional costs for running the developer extensions on the embedded ABAP cloud environment in S/4HANA Cloud. When cloud vendors run their solution as a SAAS solution on BTP, they can share the costs with multiple customers. The advantage of the SAAS solution above the Addon is that the cloud vendor fully controls the code line, and the customer is still responsible for accessing and connecting to their S/4HANA tenant.

Another significant advantage of building connectivity from S/4HANA to your cloud solution on ABAP Cloud for BTP as a SAAS solution is that you can connect to the S/4HANA cloud public edition, make your future-proof solution with one code line, and downport the connection to other ERP versions, such as the S/4HANA cloud private edition, S/4HANA on-premise, or even SAP ECC.

Figure 2 – Codeline options of ABAP Cloud

 

Because the ABAP Cloud on the BTP code line follows SAP’s Clean core strategy, it should also run as an add-on on the S/4HANA Cloud editions when these editions support the same underlying ABAP Cloud version.

As mentioned before, in SAP ECC, many SAP solution partners have already provided an add-on to solve the missing functionality of standard SAP. However, this add-on should be rewritten to be S/4HANA Cloud compliant because SAP only accepts code that follows SAP’s Clean core strategy. The SAP solution partner can rewrite the code as S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility, but in this case, the partner always depends on the customers' upgrade strategy. Suppose he wants to control the versioning of his code line and innovations. In that case, the partner can rewrite the addon as a side-by-side extensibility with SAP Build Code in SAP CAP or ABAP, which connects to S/4HANA. Or he can even run the applications in a non-SAP cloud environment of his choice and follow the extensibility options described above. From my experience, all these options are valid, future-proof, and in line with the solution for cloud vendors.

Cloud and SAP Add-on vendors who want to build their solutions with ABAP Cloud can even speed up their development, shorten the time to market, and shift responsibilities back to their customers using an OEM solution like the FEXS API Connect from fexs.com. These OEM solutions already take care of the most common tasks, resulting in less development needed, smaller SAP support departments, and higher customer satisfaction.

Conclusion

Because SAP and its customers are still on the move with S/4HANA to its future Cloud ERP landscape, it isn’t easy for SAP Solution partners to make the right decision for their future-proof solution.  The move of SAP to the cloud and the openness and flexibility of its portfolio will give SAP Addon partners and existing cloud solution providers an excellent opportunity to bring added value to the existing SAP community.  Suppose your solution strongly depends on S/4HANA processes. In that case, I believe a partner application running as S/4HANA Cloud Developer Extensibility or on a SAP BTP ABAP Environment following SAP Clean Core strategy is the best and most future-proof solution. An article is always too short to explain all the details, but I hope this article triggers your mind.  And in my job as a strategic architect and advisor, I’m open to any discussion and available to help push you in the right direction.

This article was initially posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe

entry.title: SAP Retail : Merchandise & Article Hierarchies Key Points to consider
entry.content:
SAP Retail: Merchandise Hierarchy vs. Article Hierarchy

In the SAP Retail solution, two key hierarchies exist: Merchandise Hierarchy and Article Hierarchy. Each serves distinct purposes, but confusion often arises during implementation when their roles are not clearly defined. This can lead to redundancy or over-complication in hierarchies, hampering efficiency.

  1. Merchandise Hierarchy

Purpose:

Focused on internal processes, the Merchandise Hierarchy organizes goods for procurement, pricing, and inventory management. It aligns with the merchant’s perspective to manage purchasing, vendor interactions, and promotions.

Key Features:

  • Articles are grouped by shared characteristics, e.g., Apparel → Men's Apparel → Dress Shirt → Slim Fit.
  • Attributes such as color, size, and brand are maintained at the article master level.
  • Articles are assigned to one node at the lowest level of the hierarchy.
  • Useful for:
    • Condition records and mass maintenance.
    • Procurement-focused planning and analytics.
    • Defining assortments and streamlining listing processes.

Best Practices:

  • Use GS1 standards (e.g., Segment → Family → Class → Brick → Attribute).
  • Avoid creating overly complex hierarchies to fit both procurement and sales needs.
  1. Article Hierarchy

Purpose:

Customer-focused, the Article Hierarchy reflects how consumers make purchasing decisions, often modeled as a Consumer Decision Tree (CDT).

Key Features:

  • Articles can belong to multiple hierarchies, with time-dependent structures.
  • Designed for sales analytics, profitability analysis, and retail planning.
  • Example: Men's Dress Shirt → Slim Fit → No Iron → White → Size (matching consumer search behavior).

Best Practices:

  • Maintain the hierarchy at the client level to standardize across distribution chains.
  • Limit to 5-6 levels to prevent performance issues.
  • Avoid frequent changes to maintain historical data integrity for reporting.

Practical Example

For a Shirt, the Merchandise Hierarchy might classify it as:
Apparel → Men’s Apparel → Dress Shirt → Slim Fit, with attributes like color, size, and brand.

The Article Hierarchy, however, aligns with a consumer perspective:
Men’s Dress Shirt → Slim Fit → No Iron → White → Size, catering to how customers search and shop.

Key Considerations

  1. Separate Focus: Merchandise Hierarchy is procurement-centric, while Article Hierarchy is consumer-centric.
  2. Avoid Overlap: Do not attempt to merge requirements into one hierarchy to prevent unnecessary complexity.
  3. Planning and Reporting: Use Article Hierarchy for sales-driven decisions; Merchandise Hierarchy for purchasing and inventory tasks.
  4. Sustainability: Limit levels to optimize performance and maintain historical data accuracy.
  5. Flexibility: Design Article Hierarchies to adapt to evolving consumer behaviors while keeping Merchandise Hierarchy stable.

By clearly distinguishing the roles of Merchandise and Article Hierarchies, retailers can optimize both internal operations and customer-facing processes. This ensures efficient planning, reporting, and an enhanced consumer experience.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Updated Transport to Production Strategy for Rapid Activation
entry.content:
If you are operating with SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA, you will need to transport your launchpad content, layout, and other configuration settings from your Development to your Production environment. You can now find an updated document with the latest advice on the transport strategy for SAP Fiori Rapid Activation and related SAP Fiori objects at

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Rapid Activation Transport Move to Production Strategy

 

What is SAP Fiori Rapid Activation?

As a reminder, the SAP Fiori Rapid Activation procedure lets you, as a system administrator, quickly activate the capabilities of the SAP Fiori launchpad and SAP Fiori applications using a series of task lists.

Refer to

  • As explained in the central note for all SAP S/4HANA releases, SAP Note 2902673 - Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA - Overview
  • SAP Help - SAP Fiori Overview - Rapid Activation of SAP Fiori
  • SAP Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Activate SAP Fiori in 1 day or less - the video playlist and FAQ
  • SAP Blog - SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Creating your custom business roles – the end-to-end process and video playlist

What’s changed in this version?

This document has the latest information for SAP S/4HANA 2023 release including

  • Considerations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers
  • Clearer explanation of how transport of Rapid Activation works
  • Prerequisites for transporting Rapid Activation
  • Which task lists to run on implementation versus upgrade
  • Mandatory/Optional tasks to run in Development versus subsequent systems
  • Critical parameters for transports
  • Coverage of new task list SAP_FIORI_FCM_CATALOG_ACTIVATION
  • How to transport settings in other Rapid Activation tools such as Launchpad configuration parameters in transaction /UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF

For official guidance for your SAP S/4HANA release and FPS, always refer to the Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA <release> <FPSxx>" for your SAP S/4HANA release and FPS (example: SAP Note 3469488 - Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02)

The links to these SAP notes are available in the document.

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru


You’ll find much more on the community topic page for SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Other helpful links in the SAP Community:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA


Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG

entry.title: How to enrich values in sorted table in loop-methods
entry.content:
Sometimes we need to loop over table and enriching the values via different methods. For SORTED tables we can not pass the line via CHANGING as it leads to runtime error MOVE_TO_LIT_NOTALLOWED_NODATA.

The code below (full code is here) will lead to runtime error (as on the screenshot)

 

 

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS main.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_name_val
        , key_name TYPE string
        , val TYPE string
        , add_value TYPE string
      ,  END OF ts_name_val
      , tt_name_val TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_name_val WITH DEFAULT KEY
      , tt_name_val_srt TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ts_name_val WITH UNIQUE KEY key_name
      .

    METHODS fill_add_value
      CHANGING cs_name_val TYPE ts_name_val.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD main.
    DATA lt_name_val TYPE tt_name_val_srt.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_name_val> TYPE ts_name_val.

    lt_name_val = value #(
    ( key_name = 'NUM100' val = 'base_val_100' )
    ( key_name = 'NUM102' val = 'base_val_102' )
    ).

    LOOP AT lt_name_val ASSIGNING <fs_name_val>.
      fill_add_value( CHANGING cs_name_val = <fs_name_val> ).
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD fill_add_value.
    cs_name_val-add_value = 'some val'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  new lcl_app( )->main( ).

 

 

 

However instead of changing we could pass as ref via importing parameter. The code without runtime errors is below (full code could be found here).

 

 

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS main.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_name_val
        , key_name TYPE string
        , val TYPE string
        , add_value TYPE string
      ,  END OF ts_name_val
      , tt_name_val TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_name_val WITH DEFAULT KEY
      , tt_name_val_srt TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ts_name_val WITH UNIQUE KEY key_name
      .

    METHODS fill_add_value
      CHANGING cs_name_val TYPE ts_name_val.

    METHODS fill_add_value_ref
      IMPORTING isr_name_val TYPE REF TO ts_name_val.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD main.
    DATA lt_name_val TYPE tt_name_val_srt.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs_name_val> TYPE ts_name_val.

    lt_name_val = VALUE #(
    ( key_name = 'NUM100' val = 'base_val_100' )
    ( key_name = 'NUM102' val = 'base_val_102' )
    ).

*    LOOP AT lt_name_val ASSIGNING <fs_name_val>.
*      fill_add_value( CHANGING cs_name_val = <fs_name_val> ).
*    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT lt_name_val ASSIGNING <fs_name_val>.
      fill_add_value_ref( isr_name_val = REF #( <fs_name_val> ) ).
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD fill_add_value.
    cs_name_val-add_value = 'some val'.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD fill_add_value_ref.
    "IMPORTING isr_name_val TYPE REF TO ts_name_val.
    isr_name_val->add_value = 'some val'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  NEW lcl_app( )->main( ).

 

 

But how are you using do enrich sorted table? via object attribute? Please comment and share you knowledge!
Also I want to highlight that there is a difference between Compiler analyzer and Runtime-analyzer and the only tool to deal with that - it is unit tests or component tests. Hope I could do some blogging about that 😉

Thanks for your attention! 🙂

entry.title: Situation Handling in Handling Unit Management In S/4 Hana
entry.content:
Hi Readers

Today I am here with new features in Handling Unit Management which is called situation handling. Here I will explain only conceptual knowledge for it. Please go through it and advise me to improve if it is required.

Brief Introduction to Handling Unit and its features

Handling Unit

A handling unit is a physical unit consisting of packaging materials and the goods contained on it. A handling unit is always a combination of materials and packaging materials

 

Nested Handling Unit

Handling units can be nested, and we can create a new handling unit from several other handling units.

 

Note- A handling unit has a unique, scannable identification number that we can construct according to industry standards such as GSI-128 or SSCC. For Example, Label Printing for EAN128 and Adapting the Label Layout.

We can use Handling Unit Management to map packing-controlled logistics in the SAP system. Here, we do not consider the movements of the individual materials, instead we map the movements of the handling unit  that contain these materials.
